_comment: Host x86 ground station — two RTL88x2 USB adapters, both on channel 161 HT40+. Ground initializes at HT40 (40 MHz, secondary above 161, center 5815): it decodes both 20 MHz and 40 MHz transmissions on the primary, so it works with any vehicle width. The VEHICLE (air) must stay HT20 — its rtl8812eu cannot do HT40 in monitor mode; forcing the air to HT40 corrupts its TX (verified: ~27% loss). So the live video downlink is HT20-on-HT40-channel today; HT40 ground is a strictly-more-capable, forward-compatible default. radiotap TX width (tunnel bw / SET_RADIO) stays 20 — HT40 here is purely the iw channel width.
_comment_nm: Both adapters are pinned NM-unmanaged in /etc/NetworkManager/conf.d/99-wfb-unmanaged.conf (by MAC), so no nmcli managed toggling is needed here — that toggling raced NM and intermittently left an adapter in 'managed' after 'ip link up'.

up: iw dev wlx40a5ef2f229b set monitor otherbss
iw dev wlx40a5ef2f229b set channel 161 HT40+
iw dev wlx40a5ef2f229b set txpower fixed 2000
iw dev wlx40a5ef2f2308 set monitor otherbss
iw dev wlx40a5ef2f2308 set channel 161 HT40+
iw dev wlx40a5ef2f2308 set txpower fixed 2000

_comment_telemetry: In-process udp:6700 -> sqlite logger + dashboard (replaces the Python webui_session.sh). The video rx tunnel taps stats to 127.0.0.1:6700; the logger ingests and serves the dashboard at /telemetry on the supervisor's HTTP port.

tunnels._comment: Diversity RX. udp_out omitted -> wfb_rx default 127.0.0.1:5600. Stats forwarded to uplink TX UDP-in for back-channel. keyless:true -> no -K passed; receives the vehicle's open (-xx) video downlink without any key (the global key_file still serves uplink TX + probe RX).
tunnels.name: video

tunnels._comment: Boundary-probe RX (PROBE_PER_SPEC.md). Matches the vehicle's probe wfb_tx (S99wfb, wfbprobe=1, link 50/port 50). probe:true makes the supervisor compute windowed per-MCS PER and forward {type:probe} records to stats_out (uplink back-channel) INSTEAD of raw rx_ant. udp_out MUST be an explicit dead port — never 5600 (RTP video); the supervisor rejects the config otherwise.
tunnels.name: probe

tunnels._comment: Uplink TX. -T 1 sets fec_timeout=1ms. control_port=8000 enables wfb_cmd passthrough via /api/v1/tunnels/uplink/control.
tunnels.name: uplink

tunnels._comment: Recovery backdoor TX (open/keyless -xx). Separate link id 209 carries the UNAUTHENTICATED APFPV-recovery channel, reachable even when the keyed uplink is dead (lost/mismatched drone.key). Robust low MCS (0) so it gets through in poor conditions; shares the uplink card. POST /api/v1/recovery emits WCMD_KEY_RECOVERY_APFPV here as a heavy redundancy burst; the vehicle's keyless recovery wfb_rx (link 209) + recovery_dispatch accept ONLY that one safe, deferred action. -K is passed but ignored in -xx mode.
tunnels.name: recovery
